reminders.description:
Task reminders, Each trigger string must follow this pattern:
TRIGGER(;RELATED=(END))?:(-)?P[nY][nM][nW][nD][T[nH][nM][nS]]
Explanation
    TRIGGER: — required prefix
    ;RELATED=START|END — optional, defines whether the trigger is relative to the start or end time
    - — optional, indicates the trigger occurs before the reference time (absence means after)
    P — duration designator
    nY nM nW nD — years, months, weeks, days
    T — time separator
    nH nM nS — hours, minutes, seconds.
Examples
[
  "TRIGGER:-PT60M",
  "TRIGGER:-P1DT2H",
  "TRIGGER;RELATED=END:-PT15M",
  "TRIGGER:PT0S"
]
 Notes
    "TRIGGER:-PT60M" means 60 minutes before the reference time
    "TRIGGER;RELATED=END:-PT15M" means 15 minutes before the end time
    "TRIGGER:PT0S" represents an “on-time” trigger
    This structure corresponds to a duration format similar to ISO-8601 (e.g., P1DT2H), with additional semantics for direction and reference point.
reminders.title: Reminders

repeatFlag.description:
Task repeat flag
This field MUST be a valid recurrence rule string.

Use:
- RRULE for standard recurrence
- ERULE for custom or advanced recurrence

DO NOT mix RRULE and ERULE.

Prefer examples:
- "RRULE:FREQ=DAILY"
- "RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,WE"
- "ERULE:NAME=CUSTOM;BYDATE=20260325,20260330"
repeatFlag.title: Repeatflag

items.title: Items

title.title: Title

content.description: Task content, show when kind is "TASK" or "NOTE"
content.title: Content

desc.description: Task description, show when kind is "CHECKLIST"
desc.title: Desc

dueDate.description: Task due date, show when not same as start date, mean it is duration
dueDate.title: Duedate

isAllDay.description: Whether the task is all day task
isAllDay.title: Isallday

priority.description: Task priority, 0 means no priority, 1 means low priority, 3 means medium priority, 5 means high priority
priority.title: Priority

reminders.description:
Task reminders, Each trigger string must follow this pattern:
TRIGGER(;RELATED=(END))?:(-)?P[nY][nM][nW][nD][T[nH][nM][nS]]
Explanation
    TRIGGER: — required prefix
    ;RELATED=START|END — optional, defines whether the trigger is relative to the start or end time
    - — optional, indicates the trigger occurs before the reference time (absence means after)
    P — duration designator
    nY nM nW nD — years, months, weeks, days
    T — time separator
    nH nM nS — hours, minutes, seconds.
Examples
[
  "TRIGGER:-PT60M",
  "TRIGGER:-P1DT2H",
  "TRIGGER;RELATED=END:-PT15M",
  "TRIGGER:PT0S"
]
 Notes
    "TRIGGER:-PT60M" means 60 minutes before the reference time
    "TRIGGER;RELATED=END:-PT15M" means 15 minutes before the end time
    "TRIGGER:PT0S" represents an “on-time” trigger
    This structure corresponds to a duration format similar to ISO-8601 (e.g., P1DT2H), with additional semantics for direction and reference point.
reminders.title: Reminders

repeatFlag.description:
Task repeat flag
This field MUST be a valid recurrence rule string.

Use:
- RRULE for standard recurrence
- ERULE for custom or advanced recurrence

DO NOT mix RRULE and ERULE.

Prefer examples:
- "RRULE:FREQ=DAILY"
- "RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,WE"
- "ERULE:NAME=CUSTOM;BYDATE=20260325,20260330"
repeatFlag.title: Repeatflag
